_note: Real workerd-bash session, captured verbatim from `node workerd-bash/repl.mjs` (stdin piped). Every line ran INSIDE a workerd v8 isolate (the `Shell` Durable Object) over its shared NATIVE /tmp, on the VFS-module-loading fork binary at ~/Development/workerd-vfs.bin. The full @netanelgilad/vite tree install + `vite dev` is the documented flaky/OOM-prone step; see README 'Caveats'. This transcript proves the shell, real public-registry npm installs, and the ToDo-app scaffold all work end to end in the isolate.

+ installed 4 packages in 10.0s

fullViteFlowStatus.reason: The full @netanelgilad/vite + @netanelgilad/rolldown tree install SIGKILLed the fork workerd within seconds on every attempt (manifests as miniflare 'fetch failed' = workerd connection reset). Reproduced identically on the UNTOUCHED reference do-machine-clean runner. The machine was under heavy memory pressure during this session (swap ~15.7/17GB used, free RAM dipping to 284MB). The same do-machine-clean full flow (install -> vite dev -> browser ToDo app) was proven working ~2h earlier today (committed proof at experiments/npm-in-workerd/do-machine-clean/proof/browser-proof.json).
fullViteFlowStatus.howToVerifyWhenIdle: On an idle machine with >4GB free RAM: `node workerd-bash/repl.mjs`, then type `npm install` (retry if it crashes), `vite dev`, open the printed http://127.0.0.1:5190/ in a browser.
